div1.entryFree:
Beldam or Beldame, 1) grandmother: to show the b. daughters of her daughter, Lucr. 953. shapes her sorrow to the --'s (Hecuba's) woes, Lucr. 953 the old b. earth, H4A III, 1, 32.
2) term of contempt for an old woman: old men and --s in the streets do prophesy upon it, John IV, 2, 185. b., I think we watched you at an inch, H6B I, 4, 45. --s as you are, Mcb. III, 5, 2.
